title: hibbertia glebosa subsp. glebosa
intro: Hibbertia glebosa subsp. glebosa, also known as Yellow Daisy Bush, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Hibbertia glebosa subsp. glebosa include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea or tincture, which can be used to treat various health issues, including wounds, eczema, and digestive problems.
